dialogos.texto_en: Wow...\nSuch a beautiful hall.
It's quite different\nfrom what I imagined a museum to look like.
The ceilings are so high, and it's so vibrant and nice inside.
The shape of the pillars is also interesting.\nIt looks like they branch out from the top...\nIs it a tree motif?
Correct. According to the guidebook,\nit's based on the idea of sunbeams\nshining through the Utaki.
Utaki?
Utaki refers to a shrine where ceremonies are held.\nThey can still be found in various places in Okinawa.
What kind of ceremony?
Oh, c'mon...
You should be studying the exhibit descriptions\nin detail since you're here at the museum.\nYou're missing out if you don't.
Fu-kun, you really know everything, don't you.
Not really.\nI just did some research\nsince we chose to go to Okinawa.
I mean, if you're going on a trip,\nit's only natural to do some research beforehand, right?
I did some research,\nbut there's only so much I can remember...
Jeez, what am I gonna do with you?!\nI'll make sure to teach you ALL about Okinawa!
Hey now, hey now.\nDid you forget that you're no longer our tutor anymore?
That's right,\nthere's no longer a student-teacher relationship.
Anyway, where should we start looking around from?
Should we save the special exhibition for later?\nSince we're going to see everything anyways,\nthe main attraction should be saved for last.
We don't have to stick together\nand go around in a group, right?\nYou can explore whichever exhibit you'd like to see.
Nino, aren't you just saying that so you can run off to the café\nor the museum shop?
Come on, Nino.\nSince we're here already, let's make use of this chance and explore.
Okay, fine.
Alright,\nlet's meet back here in the hall\nafter we're all done exploring.
Sure, see you later.
(...This is my chance. I'll talk to each quintuplet\n and try to find out\n if there's something that's bothering Miku!)
